question_title: Will the interest in “rory kennedy” change between 2026-03-31 and 2026-04-07 according to Google Trends?
summary: The spike in "rory kennedy" search interest was driven by FX's Love Story finale (March 26, 2026), which dramatized JFK Jr.'s death while flying to Rory Kennedy's wedding. The 9-episode series is now complete. Interest peaked at 100 on March 28 and has already decayed to 43 by March 31. Pre-spike baseline was ~6 (median). The current value of 43 is 7x above baseline and only needs to drop 4 points (to below 40) to resolve as "Decreases." Even extremely slow decay (10%/day) projects a value of ~21 by April 7. With no ongoing coverage driver, continued decay toward baseline is near-certain. Monte Carlo simulation puts Decreases at ~97%. I set final probability at 95% to leave some room for unexpected catalysts or measurement artifacts.

factors.description: FX's Love Story finale aired March 26 - the primary driver of the spike. The 9-episode series is now complete with no more episodes. The show prominently featured Rory Kennedy's connection to JFK Jr. (he died flying to her wedding). This was the main catalyst and it's now over.

Clear decay pattern already visible: 100 (Mar 28) → 82 (Mar 29) → 43 (Mar 30-31). The value has already dropped 57% from peak in 3 days. Pre-spike baseline was ~6 (median). By April 7, a full 10 days past the finale, continued decay toward baseline is near-certain.

Current value of 43 is still 7x above baseline (~6). The gap between current (43) and the 'Decreases' threshold (40) is only 3 points - even minimal decay crosses it. Every realistic decay scenario puts the Apr 7 value well below 40.

Possible counterforce: Rory Kennedy's 'Queen of Chess' documentary is a Netflix hit, which could sustain some baseline interest. However, the documentary has been out for weeks and wasn't generating significant search interest on its own before the Love Story spike.
Risk of a new catalyst (Kennedy family news, award nomination, new controversy) that could spike interest again. Low probability over any given 7-day window but nonzero.

reasoning:
**Forecast: Decreases (95%)**

The interest in "rory kennedy" will almost certainly decrease between March 31 and April 7. Here's why:

- **The catalyst is over.** The spike was driven by FX's *Love Story* series finale (March 26), which dramatized JFK Jr.'s fatal plane crash en route to Rory Kennedy's wedding. All 9 episodes have aired.
- **Decay is already underway.** Interest dropped from 100 (peak, Mar 28) → 82 → 43 in just 3 days. The pre-spike baseline was ~6.
- **The math is stark.** At 43, only a 4-point drop (to <40) triggers "Decreases." Even the slowest plausible decay (10%/day) projects ~21 by April 7. Monte Carlo simulation gives ~97% probability of Decreases.

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|------------|
| Decreases | 95% |
| Increases | 3% |
| Doesn't change | 2% |
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the interest in “rory kennedy” change between 2026-03-31 and 2026-04-07 according to Google Trends?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43001)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=rory%20kennedy&date=2026-03-08%202026-04-07](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=rory%20kennedy&date=2026-03-08%202026-04-07) for 2026-04-07 is within 3 of the value at 2026-03-31. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "rory kennedy",
  "date": "2026-03-08 2026-04-07",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
